jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in a relevant field or equivalent work experience
- Experience identifying, developing, negotiating, and closing opportunities across a wide spectrum of customer engagement levels
- 7+ years of B2B or enterprise sales with a focus on hunting new business experience
jobs.description:
Come be a part of a rapidly expanding $35 billion dollar global business. At Amazon Business, a fast-growing startup passionate about building solutions, we set out every day to innovate and disrupt the status quo. We stand at the intersection of tech & retail in the B2B space developing innovative purchasing and procurement solutions to help businesses and organizations thrive. At Amazon Business, we strive to be the most recognized and preferred strategic partner for smart business buying. Bring your insight, imagination and a healthy disregard for the impossible. Join us in building and celebrating the value of Amazon Business to buyers and sellers of all sizes and industries. Unlock your career potential.

The Amazon Business team is dedicated to developing solutions inclusive of an expanded online store that combines the selection, convenience, and value customers have come to know and love from Amazon, with new features and unique benefits tailored to customers in the Commercial sector. As a Principal Executive, Large Enterprise Growth, you will have the unique opportunity to acquire new AB customers with annual revenues > $5B. This role will work with our most strategic greenfield opportunities in close collaboration with internal partner teams to develop and implement GTM acquisition plans.

The Principal Account Executive on the Growth team has a unique role that is responsible for identifying and acquiring new customer opportunities with custom solutions that can scale. This role requires a hands-on approach from a results-oriented individual who possesses strong analytical and communication skills that will thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment. The successful candidate will possess a business acumen that enables them to drive specific engagement and interaction with C-suite executives and understand how to identify, develop, negotiate and close large, complex deals. They should also have a demonstrated ability to think strategically and analytically about business, product, and technical challenges, with the ability to build and convey compelling value propositions, and work cross-organizationally to build consensus. The candidate should be a self-starter with a proven track record of exceeding monthly and quarterly input and output goals.






Key job responsibilities
• Meet or exceed new account acquisition targets
• Own negotiations of agreements/deals and growth plans with prospects to drive business inputs
• Work collaboratively with cross-functional teams jointly charged with building, owning, and sharing financial goals and deliverables for select group of named accounts
• Relay market and customer needs back to internal Amazon teams including Product Management, Technical and Category Management teams
• Work closely with marketing, merchandising, demand generation, customer service and other key internal Amazon stakeholders
• Educate and network with key prospect and customer contacts
• Prepare and provide business reviews to the senior management team

- Experience with MS Office Suite, CRMs (Salesforce) and other systems
- Experience navigating prospective accounts from and into a senior executive level to identify new customer opportunities
- Experience managing complex customer relationships and initiatives with considerable impact where the customer need, sales strategy, and opportunity are not defined

jobs.title: Principal Account Executive, Large Enterprise, Amazon Business

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 1+ years of electrical or mechanical experience
- 1+ years’ experience with Microsoft Office products
- Ability to work on a flexible schedule including days, nights, and/or weekends.
jobs.description:
PDX is an AWS GovCloud region. As required by our contracts with the federal government, effective February 24, 2026, logical access to the AWS GovCloud region will be restricted to Amazon employees who are U.S. Citizens. (GovCloud may NOT be accessed from outside of the United States).

Join our dynamic team and become a critical guardian of technological infrastructure! As a Data Center Engineering Technician, you'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of mission-critical technology infrastructure, ensuring seamless operations that power global digital experiences. Your expertise will directly contribute to maintaining an extraordinary 99.999% uptime, keeping the digital world running smoothly and efficiently.

Engineering Technicians are Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot, Data Center Operation’s Mission Critical Facilities, which includes stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, as well as three phase electrical systems such as: switch gear, UPSs, PDUs, wet cell batteries and associated systems, CRAC, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical system, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, & motors.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: PDX is an AWS GovCloud region. As required by our contracts with the federal government, effective February 24, 2026, logical access to the AWS GovCloud region will be restricted to Amazon employees who are U.S. Citizens. (GovCloud may NOT be accessed from outside of the United States).

NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

Key job responsibilities
- Daily building rounds to monitor and record information from electrical, mechanical, and fire/life safety equipment to ensure 24/7 reliability and availability
- Monitor Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Management System (EPMS)
- Assist in troubleshooting of facility and rack-level events within internal Service Level Agreements (SLA)
- Perform rack installs and rack decommissioning within internal SLA
- Perform PM, troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and repair of electrical and mechanical equipment
- Technical writing to support change management program
- Monitor daily work requests and manage to resolution, and execute projects from conception to completion
- On-site support of contractors, sub-contractors and vendors, ensuring that all work performed is in accordance with established practices, procedures & local legislation
- Work schedule changes depending on specific site needs. Shifts can be up to 12-hours and may rotate on a predefined schedule. Some locations have on-call rotations.
- Role may support more than one location/site.

Physical Requirements:
Willingness and demonstrated ability to work in the following physical conditions with reasonable accommodations:
- Regularly lift and/or move up to 39 pounds independently
- Participate in group lifts for 40+ pounds
- Maintain balance and perform construction tasks while on a ladder
- Regularly walk, use hands and fingers, handle or feel, reach with hands and arms, stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl, climb stairs, twist, bend, work above shoulders, grasp, carry, push and pull objects into position
- Work in an environment requiring provided ear protection

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:

- Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed

- Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational

- Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility

Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.

A day in the life
This role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment preventative maintenance (PM) and troubleshooting, reporting to a site’s Data Center Facility Manager (FM). They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems such as switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAHU units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers, water systems, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire/life safety equipment, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

About the team
Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion of events fosters stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship, and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.title: Engineering Operations Technician

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 4+ years of experience in theatrical trade marketing, cinema marketing, theatrical distribution, or sales in the film/entertainment industry
- Demonstrated experience building and maintaining relationships with cinema circuit marketing teams and exhibitors
- Experience managing co-operative marketing agreements, including budget management and campaign coordination
- Experience coordinating multiple campaigns and bookings simultaneously
- Proficiency with marketing analytics tools (such as MOVIO)
- Proficiency with spreadsheets, databases, and business intelligence platforms for sales tracking and reporting
- Excellent communication and presentation skills with internal teams and external vendors and circuits
jobs.description:
Amazon MGM Studios seeks a Trade Marketing & Sales Support Manager for Theatrical Distribution across Australia/NZ who will be responsible for developing and executing co-operative trade marketing campaigns with cinema circuits while providing essential sales support to maximise commercial performance across the theatrical slate.

This dual-function role combines strategic marketing promotional partnerships with operational sales execution, working closely with exhibitors to optimise both promotional impact and booking performance. The position translates release strategies into circuit-specific marketing activations while supporting session planning, bookings management, and box office analysis. This position reports to the Heads of Australia/NZ Theatrical Marketing & Distribution.

Key job responsibilities
Trade Marketing & Exhibitor Promotional Partnerships:
• Develop and maintain strategic relationships with cinema circuit marketing teams to create effective and innovative marketing campaigns that connect target audiences for all theatrical releases
• Support the Heads of Australia/NZ Theatrical Marketing and Distribution to fulfil co-op marketing agreements with circuits, including budget arrangements, sequential activation, flighting, promotional commitments and marketing deliverables
• Coordinate joint and integrated promotional activities including in-cinema displays, website and lobby takeovers, premium format promotions, and digital signage campaigns, point of sale & concession campaigns
• Coordinate the production and delivery of circuit-specific marketing assets including standees, posters, digital content and co-branded materials
• Track and report on co-op marketing campaign effectiveness and return on investment by circuit
• Provide market intelligence from circuits to inform marketing campaign development and sales strategies
• Support competitive intelligence across ANZ and key international markets, including promotional strategies and release positioning
• Analyse correlation between marketing spend by circuit and box office and market share performance to optimise future campaigns
• Coordinate with the Int’l Marketing team on extensions of global exhibitor initiatives and PLF materials
• Manage trade marketing budgets and reconcile co-op marketing expenditures with cinema circuits including processing invoices and tracking payments for circuit marketing promotional partnerships in coordination with Finance team

Sales Support & Account Management:
• Assist in executing sales strategies across theatrical releases that align with commercial objectives
• Manage independent exhibitor bookings, holdovers, and maintain accurate records of sales terms and payments in coordination with Finance team
• Establish and cultivate strong working relationships with exhibitors, serving as a key point of contact for both marketing and sales matters

Operations & Materials Management:
• Collaborate with Sales & Operations to coordinate end-to-end delivery of materials, trailers, posters and promotional materials to exhibitors across ANZ, coordinating with tech suppliers and Content Delivery Service providers
• Manage the planning, production, classification and delivery of trailers and promotional assets across ANZ
• Assist with premiere events, special screenings and experiential marketing activations with cinema circuits across ANZ
• Create and implement strategic framework and execution of internal and external screening programs in collaboration with marketing, publicity, and social.
• Support the development and implementation of exhibitor conventions, trade shows and industry events in Australia, including co-ordination and production of materials, on-site activations and presentation keynotes.
jobs.description_short: Amazon MGM Studios seeks a Trade Marketing & Sales Support Manager for Theatrical Distribution across Australia/NZ who will be responsible for developing and executing co-operative trade marketing campaigns

jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Deep knowledge of the Australian/NZ cinema circuit landscape, exhibitor marketing capabilities, and theatrical exhibition market dynamics
- Experience with film marketing campaigns and understanding of theatrical release strategies
- Experience coordinating premiere events and talent appearances alongside marketing, social, and PR teams
- Familiarity with box office analytics and ability to correlate marketing spend with performance outcomes
- Knowledge of theatrical windows and release planning considerations
- Experience with business intelligence tools (Tableau, Power BI, Comscore, Numero)
- Experience coordinating and representing a studio at industry events and conventions

Acknowledgement of country:

jobs.title: Trade Marketing & Sales Support Manager, Amazon MGM Studios

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience using data and metrics to drive improvements
- Experience managing or working within cross-functional marketing and creative teams
- Experience building and optimizing multiple, simultaneous marketing campaigns
- Experience in non internship marketing
- Experience managing creator marketing campaigns
jobs.description:
Content & pop-culture junkies at heart, we passionately believe that the best stories empower people and drive meaningful conversation, one episode at a time. Our ambition is to connect authentically with customers through topical & locally-nuanced stories that entertain, delight and inspire. In this role, you will be responsible for planning, creating, iterating and distributing bespoke editorial content in various formats through social media platforms. You will understand how to bridge quantitative inputs (the science) with the qualitative characteristics of content, creators and talent (the art), and you will be expected to use those skills to make informed decisions and engage influencers in our marketing initiatives.

We are looking for an Creator Manager to join our team. In this role, you will be part of a dedicated group within the Marketing org that maintains relationships with influencers and fans of our series, films and brand. The team works collaboratively with marketing, PR, media, promotions, and partnerships to increase engagement and reach for Amazon titles and brand. As the Creator Marketing Manager, you will lead sourcing and maintaining an 'always-on' relationship with influencer talent, and create impactful creator marketing campaigns for brand and titles.

Key job responsibilities
- Create impactful creator marketing campaigns across Prime Video brand initiatives and Amazon MGM Studios titles
- Develop and maintain direct relationships with creators
- Partner with talent, influencers and Prime Video internal teams to create impactful campaigns that drive social conversation
- Drive regular communication and engagement with influencers
- Brief and manage a roster of creative, production, influencer, and social media agencies
- Oversee creator partnerships balancing channel efficiencies/target, talent goals and content creative across titles and brand campaigns
- Collaborate with a range of internal teams (e.g. PR, legal, creative, Amazon synergy) to negotiate agreements and amplify impact
- Maintain knowledge and expertise in the creator marketing economy and have a pulse on trends and leaders in the social community
- Partner with key cross-functional teams to influence successful, measurable marketing ideas, and plans while integrating influencer participation in our marketing, advertising, owned and operated channels and events
- Build strong relationships and become a critical partner to the internal Prime Video series, film brand and PR leads
- Create reports with data and insights pertaining to creator marketing campaigns
- Develop KPIs and insights both internally and with external partners to benchmark expectations and goals

About the team
Prime Video & Amazon MGM Studios is the home for talent, creating and producing Original films and television series for a global audience. Original series premiere exclusively on Prime Video, which is available in more than 240 countries and territories worldwide. Amazon MGM Studios also produces and acquires Original movies for theatrical release and exclusively for Prime Video. From award-winning shows and films like Fallout, Saltburn, The Boys, American Fiction, and The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, Amazon is building a world class entertainment offering, and we are looking for someone with an entertainment marketing background who can support our growth.
jobs.description_short: Content & pop-culture junkies at heart, we passionately believe that the best stories empower people and drive meaningful conversation, one episode at a time. Our ambition is to connect authentically with
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Experience in multi-territory campaign management

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. jobs.title: Creator Manager - 12 month Fixed Term Contract, Prime Video

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of tax, finance or a related analytical field experience
- Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, business, economics, or a related analytical field (e.g., engineering, math, computer science), or MBA and 5+ years of finance or a related analytical field experience
jobs.description:
Everything we do at Amazon is focused on being "Earth's Most Customer-Centric Company”. At the core of this customer centricity is the Worldwide Customer Service organization. Our Finance leaders work cross functionally, help others understand financial decisions and are trusted business advisors. They employ every facet of data and communication to ensure our partners and our leaders have the most up to date and reliable information to help them make the best possible decision. Come teach us a few things, and we’ll teach you a few things as we navigate the most customer-centric company on Earth.

The Amazon Customer Service Finance team is looking for a Finance Manager to join our growing business and partner with the Product & Technology teams building the next generation AI tools to serve our Customers and improve Customer Experience. This is a high-visibility role where you will partner directly with senior and executive leaders across CS to shape financial strategy in a fast-moving, ambiguous environment. You will work at the intersection of finance strategy and AI-driven innovation — building mechanisms from scratch, not inheriting someone else's playbook. The successful candidate will be a self-starting builder who thrives in ambiguity, brings strong business partnership instincts, and can navigate organizational complexity while driving results with limited precedent. You will partner with finance and business leaders across several teams to provide strategic guidance, tactical direction, and decision support.

Key job responsibilities
- Build and own financial modeling for multi-million dollar investment decisions, partnering with the business on inputs and design, including designing and managing mechanisms to enable performance monitoring.
- Partner directly with senior business leaders to deliver financial insights that drive strategic decisions across CS.
- Lead business reviews, presenting financial summaries and insights to CS Leadership.
- Navigate ambiguity across organizational changes, shifting priorities, and evolving business charters — driving clarity where none exists.
- Champion and leverage AI/automation tools to drive productivity gains and build scalable finance processes.
- Proactively design mechanisms to enable performance monitoring.
- Synthesize large volumes of data and present findings to senior management, leading with conclusions and recommendations
- Lead cross-functional workstreams across Product, Tech, Science, and Finance — driving requirements agreement, gaining buy-in from partner teams, and designing and executing plans to drive adoption of changes
- Provide mentorship to junior team members and participate in team-wide educational efforts.

A day in the life
- Participate in and drive WBRs
- Drive roadmap discussions with business partners, influencing roadmaps through data-driven insights and financial judgment
- Run MFRs, drive cost and CX improvement discussions and tradeoffs
- Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to evaluate AI-driven initiatives and quantify their impact on customer experience and cost-to-serve
- Build and iterate on finance mechanisms in a high-growth, process-light environment

jobs.title: Finance Manager, Amazon Customer Service

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 7+ years of technical product or program management experience
- 10+ years of working directly with engineering teams experience
- 5+ years of software development experience
- Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, building processes and coordinating release schedules
jobs.description:
The EC2 Demand and Forecasting team is looking for a Technical Program Manager to facilitate planning EC2 infrastructure. This role will require the candidate to work with internal customers to optimize their consumption of EC2 instances. Specifically, this individual will impact the overall Amazon CapEx plan by: working with the hundreds of AWS services & SDO teams to adopt different/new instances, creating new metrics to monitor the efficiency of team's use of EC2, and partnering with SDE teams in different orgs to decrease qualification times. This role works on a team of several other planners who divide the software teams into portfolios which allows the candidate to dive deep into the technical aspects of AWS products. The ideal candidate is one who has worked at the intersection of software engineering teams, finance, and supply chains; who can understand constraints from each group and work between worlds to create strategic plans to ensure EC2’s continued growth while optimizing for long-term efficiency.


Key job responsibilities
- Influencing other engineering organizations to make changes to their infrastructure roadmap.
- Matching supply of EC2 positions and server materials from supply chain partners to the types of demand that fit within those positions
- Quickly responding to changes in the roadmap based on supply availability, customer demand, or hardware engineering constraints.

A day in the life
Our team operates on a bi-monthly planning cycle, so we expect to spend ~2 weeks creating a new plan, ~2 weeks adjusting the plan based on feedback from our downstream partners, and ~4 weeks monitoring, tuning, and evaluating the plan. In addition, we expect this role to work closely with SDE teams that are concurrently developing new software to help increase planning effectiveness.

About the team
The EC2 Demand and Forecasting team is responsible for building forecasts/plans that drive almost all of the CapEx spend for AWS, and will directly impact the purchase of $billions of server components, data center power, and networking equipment. The team is primarily composed of TPMs but we are the primary customer of several SDE teams in EC2 and AWS Infrastructure who software to improve the planning process.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree (BA/BS) in architecture or interior design
• 5+ years of commercial retail design or architecture experience
• Intermediate Revit skills required
jobs.description:
This store design position will be part of the Amazon Worldwide Retail Growth & Development. We are an exciting, fast-moving business unit, where everyone on the team needs to be entrepreneurial, wear many hats and work effectively in a highly collaborative environment. You are the right person for this job if you are a creative problem solver with curiosity and drive for learning and a passion for customers. You must demonstrate the ability to efficiently drive multiple projects throughout all phases of design, permitting and construction administration. We are looking for people who want to join an ambitious, innovative and customer obsessed program.

Key job responsibilities
• Solutions oriented, proactive and flexible attitude; willingness to take an “all hands-on deck” approach
• Translating brand design standards and concepts in an innovative yet fiscally responsible way
• Working across a large portfolio and multiple banners with a diverse set of programs
• Manage the design process, interfacing with internal and external design partners
• Adapting site specific conditions metrics and standards to store designs
• Responsible for building data driven scope of work with internal stake holders, focused on servicing operating stores
• Responsible for development of FF&E and Décor packages
• Work with key internal and external stakeholders in the development and production of comprehensive design development packages
• Create presentation packages to communicate design with cross-functional teams and senior leadership
• Drive and create Schematic Design Packages
• Coordinate with Construction, Procurement and Operations Teams on budget and schedule considerations and value engineering efforts
• Manage consultants throughout all phases of design through the project lifecycle
• Provide Construction Administrative support as needed
• Archive completed drawings, redlines, and shop drawings
• Obtain landlord approvals per lease requirements
• Review and approves submittals and shop drawings
• Demonstrated ability in building and health codes, 3D visualization, and industry trends
• Demonstrated ability to communicate design vision in visual, written, and verbal formats
• The ability to manage multiple simultaneous projects requiring frequent communication, organization, time management, and problem-solving skills
• The ability to work effectively in an extremely fast paced, ambiguous environment


A day in the life
If you thrive in an ever-changing progressive environment, then we want to talk to you. This position will work closely with cross-functional teams, external consultants and vendors throughout the store development process. Oversight of consultant performance is critical. This candidate must have a professional and positive demeanor and possess the ability to communicate effectively with business partners at every level, including leadership.

About the team
The Worldwide Retail Growth & Development Design team approaches every site with customer obsession from the ground up to offer seamless grocery shopping experiences, whether customers are shopping in-store or online. We are at the forefront of revolutionizing a diverse grocery store network transforming the shopping experience through innovative store design. Our thoughtful designs work backwards form the customer to create experiences that transform everyday grocery shopping into an effortless journey. We optimize store layouts to create intuitive and efficient shopping environments while ensuring our designs are customer obsessed and prioritize workflow efficiency.

jobs.title: Store Designer, Worldwide Grocery Real Estate

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- High school or equivalent diploma
- 1+ years of computer/server hardware troubleshooting or related IT experience
- Ability to work on a flexible schedule including days, nights, and/or weekends.
jobs.description:
Join our dynamic AWS team and become a critical guardian of global cloud infrastructure! You'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of the world's most innovative technology platform, ensuring seamless data center operations that power millions of businesses and services worldwide.

Data Center Technicians should be willing to work both independently and with a team. Work prioritization, organizational skills, effective communication, and the ability to react quickly are critical to success. In addition to hardware and network repair, candidates will install equipment, create documentation, innovate solutions, and fix problems within the data center space. This team works in an environment that operates 24/7. Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: PDX is an AWS GovCloud region. As required by our contracts with the federal government, effective February 24, 2026, logical access to the AWS GovCloud region will be restricted to Amazon employees who are U.S. Citizens. (GovCloud may NOT be accessed from outside of the United States).

NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

Key job responsibilities
Performing server rack installations, hardware break-fix on various components, troubleshooting network issues, and responding to operational incidents that impact service availability, escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required

Serving as a primary contact point for both internal and external stakeholders, including engineers, software developers, vendors, and contractors

Performing on-call duties and participating in scheduled maintenance and change management activities

Contributing to documentation and process improvement initiatives based on your analysis of operational issues

Helping to train and onboard new team members

Physical requirements:

Regularly lift and/or move up to 40 pounds; and participate in group lifts for 41+ pounds

Working in cramped and/or elevated locations

Bending, lifting, stretching, and reaching

Standing and walking for up to 8+ hours a day

Ascending and descending ladders, stairs, and gangways safely and without limitation

Work in a noisy environment

Work shifts longer than 8 hours to support 24/7 operations (covering both night and day shifts).

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:

Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed

Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational

Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility

Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.

A day in the life
As a Data Center Technician professional, you have industry-leading technical abilities and demonstrate a breadth of knowledge while you:

Take ownership of technical issues brought by their customer base, engaging other teams when needed to drive resolution

Solve problems at their root and step back to understand the broader context

Maintain service level agreements through the implementation of proactive issue detection and reporting

You will be required to work shift work that will include days/nights/weekends/holidays. Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required. You will be responsible for having a reliable personal vehicle and valid driver's license to travel within the regional work area, as company transportation will not be provided.

About the team
Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.


Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
